message:
asupersync: continue dropping stale underscore prefixes in tracing macros

Three more instances of the same pattern — underscore-prefixed
identifiers passed to tracing macros where the binding itself
doesn't have an underscore prefix, so the macro fails to resolve
them under any build that actually evaluates the arguments (e.g.
the `tracing-integration` feature).

Changes:

* src/record/task.rs: `TaskRecord::request_cancel` (the
  Created/Running branch) was capturing
  `let _prev_state = self.state_name();` and then referencing it
  as `old_state = _prev_state` in a `tracing_compat::debug!`.
  Rename the local to `prev_state` and update the macro reference
  to match.

* src/runtime/state.rs: the task-cleanup-from-runtime-state
  `debug!` event uses `outcome_kind = _outcome_kind, waiter_count
  = _waiter_count, ...`. Both bindings exist in scope without
  underscore prefixes; drop the underscores in the macro
  arguments.

* src/types/budget.rs: `Budget::consume`'s "insufficient quota"
  path matched `Some(_remaining) => { trace!(cost_remaining =
  _remaining, ...) }`. Change the match arm to
  `Some(remaining) => { trace!(cost_remaining = remaining, ...) }`
  so the binding name matches the macro reference.

No behavior change in any of the three sites; the bindings are
only consumed by tracing macros.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
